example_prompt: Update an existing Flutterwave customer's phone number and postal address.

input_schema.description: All body fields are optional. Email is NOT updatable through this endpoint — only name, phone, address and meta are accepted.

id.description: Unique customer identifier to update (path parameter, e.g. cus_3XarBILKQS).

name.description: Optional structured name update. Each component must match ^(?![ ,.'-]*$)[A-Za-z ,.'-]{2,50}$.

phone.description: Optional structured phone update. When provided, both fields are required.

number.description: Local subscriber number (7-10 digits, digits only).

address.description: Optional postal address update. When provided, line1, city, state, postal_code and country are required.

meta.description: Optional metadata update. Replaces the existing meta object — there is no per-key merge.

created_datetime.description: Original creation timestamp — the update endpoint returns the same created_datetime, not a separate updated_datetime.

gotchas: Flutterwave v4 uses OAuth2 client credentials. POST client_id and client_secret as form-encoded body to https://idp.flutterwave.com/realms/flutterwave/protocol/openid-connect/token to obtain an access_token (valid 10 minutes), then use it as Bearer in the Authorization header. The FLUTTERWAVE_CLIENT_CREDENTIALS env var stores base64(client_id:client_secret).
Email is NOT updatable via this endpoint — the PUT body only accepts name, phone, address and meta. To change a customer's email, create a new customer record.
Always send an X-Trace-Id header (12-255 chars). X-Idempotency-Key is also recommended on PUT /customers/{id} so a retried request after a network error does not double-apply the update.
Customer IDs are opaque strings (e.g. cus_3XarBILKQS). Do not apply encodeURIComponent to the id when constructing the URL — pass it as-is.
Sending a 'meta' object replaces the full existing meta object — there is no per-key merge. Read the customer first, then send the merged meta to avoid losing existing keys.

name.first / name.middle / name.last must each match ^(?![ ,.'-]*$)[A-Za-z ,.'-]{2,50}$ (letters, spaces, commas, periods, apostrophes, hyphens; 2-50 chars; cannot be only punctuation). phone.country_code: 1-3 digits, phone.number: 7-10 digits, both digits only. address.country: uppercase ISO 3166-1 alpha-2.
Validation error items use the key 'field_name' (not 'field') — match on error.validation_errors[].field_name when surfacing per-field errors to users.
